# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

Cicada exists because good tools should be accessible to everyone — not just people with fast machines, premium subscriptions, or time to learn complex software. We extend that same accessibility to how we work together.

Everyone who participates in this project is expected to make participation a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, background, disability, experience level, nationality, or identity.

## Our Standards

**Behavior that contributes to a positive environment:**

- Being kind and patient, especially with people who are new to open source
- Giving specific, actionable feedback
- Accepting feedback without defensiveness
- Focusing on the work, not the person
- Remembering that not everyone has the same context or experience level

**Behavior that is not acceptable:**

- Harassment, intimidation, or discrimination of any kind
- Insulting or condescending comments, especially toward beginners
- Personal or political attacks
- Publishing anyone's private information without permission
- Any conduct that would reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies to all project spaces — GitHub issues, pull requests, and discussions — and to any public representation of the project.

## Enforcement

Report unacceptable behavior by opening a GitHub issue or contacting the maintainer directly. All reports will be handled promptly and with discretion. The maintainer reserves the right to close issues, reject pull requests, or block accounts that violate this Code of Conduct.

## Attribution

Adapted from the [Contributor Covenant](https://www.contributor-covenant.org), version 2.1.
